title: [CI:BUILD] Fix multiarch manifest-list build failures
body:
/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Increase the timeout interval and minimum download-rate required for the dnf command when building multi-arch manifest-list images for buildah.

#### How to verify it

Test builds will complete w/o failure

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

Fixes #4472

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

[Example failure](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4702377224175616?logs=main)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [cevich] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM, test failure looks to be a network flake.
Oops, sorry @TomSweeneyRedHat, please ignore this PR for now.  I should have WIP'd it.
Note to self: This seems like an emulation-induced timeout, when <4 arches build, it runs fine.  Simply increasing the dnf timeout should fix this.
Okay, I think I've finally licked this problem.  Knock-on-wood :fist_raised: the tests will all pass.

LGTM
and happy green test buttons
Sigh. Yes, @Luap99 is correct, this broke CI. Let's please never, ever do a VM-bump with any skip-CI directives.

Ugh, I totally forgot there was an image-update commit here at all.  So even more belt-and-suspenders: Only do image-updates in dedicated PRs, never mixed in with other random questionably-related crap :cry:
